text.title: Chunking Document Structure — Core Rules
text.summary: 5 rules for Markdown / HTML chunking: header_path propagation, min_chunk_size guard, BeautifulSoup for HTML, oversized-block subdivision, version on strategy change.

rule.statement: Every Markdown chunk MUST carry header_path = ancestor titles joined by " > ". When a section exceeds max_chunk_size and is sub-split, every sub-chunk MUST inherit the same header_path.
rule.rationale: Header_path preserves the navigational anchor inside the embedding; recall on "what does the auth section say about tokens" rises markedly when section title is present in the chunk text.

rule.statement: Chunks below min_chunk_size (default 100 tokens) MUST be merged with the prior chunk under the same header_path, or dropped if at section boundary.

rationale.source: K-NN noise analysis on shallow docs

rule.rationale: Single-line list items embed as near-zero vectors that pollute nearest-neighbour results without contributing useful signal.

rule.statement: HTML chunking MUST use BeautifulSoup (or a DOM parser); regex over HTML is forbidden for production indexing.
rule.rationale: Regex over HTML mis-handles nested tags, attribute quoting, script/style blocks. DOM walk is the only safe path.

rule.statement: When a single section/block exceeds max_chunk_size, it MUST be sub-split via the recursive splitter from chunking-basics, with each sub-chunk carrying the same header_path and an additional part_index field.
rule.rationale: Single huge sections (e.g. 8k-token API reference) silently truncate in the embedding call; sub-splitting prevents loss.

rule.statement: Changing header-depth policy or min_chunk_size MUST bump strategy_version and write to a versioned collection.
rule.rationale: Policy changes invalidate existing chunk distributions; mixing breaks A/B comparisons.
